READING A FIGMA FILE PROGRAMMATICALLY

Chapter 3 — Reading a Figma File Program — The tree has a consistent shape at the top:

beats.text: A Figma file is a tree. Every visible object on the canvas is a node. Every invisible container , frames, groups, pages , is a node. The document itself is a node: the root. The tree has a consistent shape at the top:

segments.label: 01 / 13 · HOOK

title: WHERE VARIABLES
LIVE.
segments.body_html:
Variables are not embedded in the node tree. This surprises people. They appear at the top level of the file response, a

Where Variables Live —

beats.text: Variables are not embedded in the node tree. This surprises people. They appear at the top level of the file response, alongside the document, in their own section.

segments.label: 02 / 13 · WHERE VARIABLES LIVE

title: COMPONENTS AND STYLES:
TWO PLACES EACH
segments.body_html:
Components appear in two places in the response, for two different purposes.

Components and Styles: Two Places Each — At the top level, components is a flat index — a map from component ID to metadata. Each entry has the component's name,

beats.text: Components appear in two places in the response, for two different purposes. At the top level, components is a flat index , a map from component ID to metadata. Each entry has the component's name, description, stable library key, and the ID of its component set if it belongs to a variant group. This is what you use for a component inventory: you iterate the map, you do not need to touch the document tree at all.

segments.label: 03 / 13 · COMPONENTS AND STYLES: TWO PLA

segments.section: WHAT IS NOT IN THE RESPONSE
title: WHAT IS NOT IN
THE RESPONSE.
segments.body_html:
Document node tree — Yes — document key
Components flat index — Yes — components key
Styles flat index — Yes — styles key
Variables and collections — Only on Enterprise plan — variables key

beats.text: Prototype interactions are absent. The connections between frames, the trigger types, the animation settings, the smart-animate configurations , none of this is in the document graph returned by GET /v1/files/:key. It exists in the Figma editor, but it does not come through the REST surface this chapter works with. Font files are absent. You can see which font families and weights are in use by inspecting the style properties on TEXT nodes. You cannot download the fonts through the Figma API.

segments.label: 04 / 13 · WHAT IS NOT IN THE RESPONSE

title: FIGMA-READ.MJS
segments.body_html:

beats.text: The tool that makes this chapter concrete is figma-read.mjs. It fetches the file, writes a local fixture, and produces three outputs: a component inventory, a variable inventory, and a missing-description report. It is the second named CLI artifact in this book. The .mjs extension signals ES module format. Node 18 and later support top-level await in ES modules , use it here rather than wrapping everything in an immediately-invoked async function.

segments.label: 05 / 13 · FIGMA-READ.MJS

title: THE LOCAL
FIXTURE.
segments.body_html:
The fixture written by figma-read.mjs is not a nice-to-have. It is a core practice.

The Local Fixture — A full file fetch on a large design system takes several seconds and consumes rate-limit quota. Figma imposes rate limit

beats.text: The fixture written by figma-read.mjs is not a nice-to-have. It is a core practice. A full file fetch on a large design system takes several seconds and consumes rate-limit quota. Figma imposes rate limits on the REST API . If you are iterating on extraction logic , debugging a variable resolver, adjusting a traversal, testing a new output format , hitting the live API on every run means slow feedback loops and the real possibility of exhausting your quota mid-session.

segments.label: 07 / 13 · THE LOCAL FIXTURE

title: THREE READ
STRATEGIES.
segments.body_html:
GET /v1/files/key — Full audits, complete traversals, any analysis where you do not know in advance which nodes you need
GET /v1/files/key?depth=N — Structural reads: page names, top-level frame types, checking node presence without traversing subtrees
GET /v1/files/key/nodes?ids=ID1,ID2,... — Targeted extraction: you have a list of component IDs from a prior structural pass and need

beats.text: Not every question about a Figma file requires the full 47,000-line response. There are three read strategies, each suited to different questions. GET /v1/files/:key , the full fetch , returns the complete document tree, all component metadata, all style metadata, and the variables block. Use this when you need to traverse the full graph: complete audits, compliance checks, any analysis where you do not know in advance which nodes you will care about. Accept that this is slow on large files. Write the fixture after the first fetch.

segments.label: 08 / 13 · THREE READ STRATEGIES

title: THE OUTPUT
SHAPE.
segments.body_html:
figma-read.mjs produces three files. These are the stable interface for everything downstream.

The Output Shape — component-inventory.json is a flat list of all component definitions: names, IDs, descriptions, and a boolean hasDescrip

beats.text: figma-read.mjs produces three files. These are the stable interface for everything downstream. component-inventory.json is a flat list of all component definitions: names, IDs, descriptions, and a boolean hasDescription. It is consumed by documentation sync in Chapter 10, audit tooling in Chapter 5, and specification generation in Chapter 12.

segments.label: 09 / 13 · THE OUTPUT SHAPE

title: FAILURE MODES
segments.body_html: The large-file timeout.→The partial variable response.→Stale node IDs.→The archive page problem.→Instance vs. definition confusion.

beats.text: Understanding how this script fails is as important as understanding how it works. The large-file timeout. For files with hundreds of components and deep nesting, the full fetch can take a long time , sometimes tens of seconds. If the connection drops before the response completes, you get an error rather than a partial response. There is no partially-delivered JSON here; the API returns the full body or nothing.

segments.label: 10 / 13 · FAILURE MODES

title: WHAT COMES
NEXT.
segments.body_html:

What Comes Next — The gap that remains is not about reading. It is about trust. You can extract what the file contains; you cannot yet det

beats.text: You can now read any Figma file programmatically. You have a fixture for offline development. You have a component inventory, a variable inventory, and a missing-description report , three files that are the stable input for every downstream tool in this book. The gap that remains is not about reading. It is about trust. You can extract what the file contains; you cannot yet determine whether the file is structured in a way that a pipeline can rely on.

segments.label: 11 / 13 · WHAT COMES NEXT
